ResearchBlog

Shubham Kumar | Personal Website

Shubham Kumar

Welcome to my website! I am a third-year PhD student in the Computer Vision and Robotics Laboratory at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, advised by Prof. Narendra Ahuja.

I want to understand how and why AI models (for any modality) fail. I believe the key to getting there is by making sense of our model's intermediate representations. I am currently at IBM this summer, under the mentorship of Saurabh Jha.

I obtained my B.S. from UCSD, where I did research with Prof. Truong Nguyen and Prof. Pamela Cosman.

Email / LinkedIn / GitHub / Scholar / YouTube

Recent News

  • May 2026: Joined IBM as an extern for the summer.
  • April 2026: Check out our pre-print on explaining successful jailbreak attacks on LLMs.
  • February 2026: Our paper, SURF, was accepted to CVPR 2026.
  • May 2025: Passed my PhD qualifying exam!
  • August 2023: Arrived in Champaign, IL to start my PhD journey :)
  • June 2023: Graduated Summa Cum Laude from UCSD.
  • April 2023: Recieved the NSF Graduate Research Fellowship.
  • March 2023: Commited to UIUC for a MS/PhD program. Recieved the Illinois Distinguished Fellowship, ECE Distinguished Research Fellowship, and ECE Promise of Excellence Fellowship.

Research Highlights

Measuring the (Un)Faithfulness of Concept-Based Explanations

CVPR 2026
In our CVPR 2026 paper, we propose SURF, a principled faithfulness measure for unsupervised concept-based explanations. We show that many prior explanation methods are not as faithful as previously reported.

View project →
